heading: Privacy protection for customer information of financial institutions
label: 6821 Privacy protection for customer information of financial institutions

children.heading: Prohibition on obtaining customer information by false pretenses
children.label: a Prohibition on obtaining customer information by false pretenses

children.text: by making a false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement or representation to an officer, employee, or agent of a financial institution;
by making a false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement or representation to a customer of a financial institution; or

by providing any document to an officer, employee, or agent of a financial institution, knowing that the document is forged, counterfeit, lost, or stolen, was fraudulently obtained, or contains a false, fictitious, or fraudulent statement or representation.

children.heading: Prohibition on solicitation of a person to obtain customer information from financial institution under false pretenses
children.label: b Prohibition on solicitation of a person to obtain customer information from financial institution under false pretenses
children.text: It shall be a violation of this subchapter to request a person to obtain customer information of a financial institution, knowing that the person will obtain, or attempt to obtain, the information from the institution in any manner described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Nonapplicability to law enforcement agencies
children.label: c Nonapplicability to law enforcement agencies
children.text: No provision of this section shall be construed so as to prevent any action by a law enforcement agency, or any officer, employee, or agent of such agency, to obtain customer information of a financial institution in connection with the performance of the official duties of the agency.

children.heading: Nonapplicability to financial institutions in certain cases
children.label: d Nonapplicability to financial institutions in certain cases

children.text: testing the security procedures or systems of such institution for maintaining the confidentiality of customer information;
investigating allegations of misconduct or negligence on the part of any officer, employee, or agent of the financial institution; or
recovering customer information of the financial institution which was obtained or received by another person in any manner described in subsection (a) or (b).

children.heading: Nonapplicability to insurance institutions for investigation of insurance fraud
children.label: e Nonapplicability to insurance institutions for investigation of insurance fraud
children.text: No provision of this section shall be construed so as to prevent any insurance institution, or any officer, employee, or agency of an insurance institution, from obtaining information as part of an insurance investigation into criminal activity, fraud, material misrepresentation, or material nondisclosure that is authorized for such institution under State law, regulation, interpretation, or order.

children.heading: Nonapplicability to certain types of customer information of financial institutions
children.label: f Nonapplicability to certain types of customer information of financial institutions
children.text: No provision of this section shall be construed so as to prevent any person from obtaining customer information of a financial institution that otherwise is available as a public record filed pursuant to the securities laws (as defined in ).section 78c(a)(47) of this title

children.heading: Nonapplicability to collection of child support judgments
children.label: g Nonapplicability to collection of child support judgments
children.text: No provision of this section shall be construed to prevent any State-licensed private investigator, or any officer, employee, or agent of such private investigator, from obtaining customer information of a financial institution, to the extent reasonably necessary to collect child support from a person adjudged to have been delinquent in his or her obligations by a Federal or State court, and to the extent that such action by a State-licensed private investigator is not unlawful under any other Federal or State law or regulation, and has been authorized by an order or judgment of a court of competent jurisdiction.
